title: cistus × laxus var. subhirsutus
intro: Cistus × laxus var. subhirsutus, also known as Rock Rose, is a hybrid plant species in the Cistus genus, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Cistus × laxus var. subhirsutus include its ability to promote skin healing, reduce stress, and improve respiratory health. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
